new_classes.label_en: Other Vinegar (not wine vinegar) in containers > 2 L (CN 2209 00 99)
new_classes.label_de: Anderer Essig (kein Weinessig) in Behältnissen mit mehr als 2 L Inhalt (KN 2209 00 99)
new_classes.definition_en: Vinegar other than wine vinegar, presented in containers with a nominal volume greater than 2 litres, as described in HS Explanatory Notes to heading 2209, Part I second paragraph points 2, 3 and 4 and Part II. This is the sibling of CN 2209 00 91 (small containers ≤ 2 L).

coverage_explanation: The two main discriminating criteria for CN 2209 00 99 are: (1) the vinegar is NOT wine vinegar (captured by complement restriction against WineVinegar); (2) it is presented in containers with a volume greater than 2 litres (captured by the someValuesFrom restriction on BFO:has_part → LargeContainer). The sibling disjointness with OtherVinegarSmallContainer (2209 00 91) is also expressed. Coverage is partial (0.75) because: the legal note references HS Explanatory Notes Part I second paragraph points 2, 3 and 4 and Part II, which enumerate specific product types of other vinegar (e.g. spirit vinegar, cider vinegar, malt vinegar, vinegar substitutes from acetic acid) — these fine-grained type distinctions are not individually modelled in the current TBox and no dedicated quality classes or process classes for these sub-types exist. The AceticFermentationProcess class exists in the TBox but is not linked here as it would be too broad; the legal note does not restrict this code purely to acetic-fermentation-derived vinegar. The complement restriction pattern used here is an approximation for disjointness, as OWL complement on a has_part restriction is a modelling shortcut rather than a precise expression; the container-volume discrimination is precisely captured via the LargeContainer class.
